context.label: New Jedi Order / Yuuzhan Vong War

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends New Jedi Order era, from Vector Prime and the first Yuuzhan Vong invasion through The Unifying Force and the war settlement. Excludes Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Disney canon outcomes except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and later post-NJO Legends unless a later deck is active.

content.fact: Yuuzhan Vong society is shaped by gods, sacrifice, caste, pain, honor, and biological technology.
content.injection: Write Yuuzhan Vong culture as theocratic and bio-organic; do not treat them as ordinary Imperial aliens with metal ships. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Jedi Order / Yuuzhan Vong War.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development Lorecard pending human review.

content.fact: Warriors, priests, shapers, intendants, workers, and shamed ones occupy different roles and status.
content.injection: When writing Yuuzhan Vong scenes, choose caste role and status; do not flatten them into identical warriors. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Jedi Order / Yuuzhan Vong War.

content.fact: Shamed Ones are stigmatized within Yuuzhan Vong society and become important to internal unrest.
content.injection: Use Shamed Ones as a pressure point inside Vong society, not as ordinary soldiers. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Galactic Alliance transition.

entries.title: Old New Republic Politics Fail Under Invasion

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends New Jedi Order era, from Vector Prime and the first Yuuzhan Vong invasion through The Unifying Force and the war settlement. Excludes Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Disney canon outcomes except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and later post-NJO Legends unless a later deck is active.

content.fact: Old New Republic structures prove too slow and factional for the Yuuzhan Vong crisis.
content.injection: Before the Galactic Alliance forms, show old institutions struggling with denial, blame, jurisdiction, and refugee overload.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Early Yuuzhan Vong invasion.
Respect story-position bounds from Vector Prime / Invasion Begins to Ithor Devastated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: The Galactic Alliance is a wartime coalition structure trying to replace failed New Republic legitimacy.
content.injection: After its formation, use coalition politics, military necessity, and Jedi negotiation as default governmental pressures.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Galactic Alliance Forms.
Respect story-position bounds from Galactic Alliance Forms to Galactic Alliance Forms.

content.fact: The Peace Brigade is a collaborator network rather than a stable galactic state.
content.injection: Use Peace Brigade for betrayal, bounties, and opportunist collaboration; do not make it a normal government. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends New Jedi Order era, from Vector Prime and the first Yuuzhan Vong invasion through The Unifying Force and the war settlement. Excludes Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Disney canon outcomes except as future guards.

content.fact: The Jedi Order is publicly contested during the Yuuzhan Vong War, with some seeing Jedi as protectors and others as magnets for disaster.
content.injection: In NJO public scenes, let Jedi status be socially unstable rather than universally revered. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi under siege before Myrkr.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Hunt Escalates to Myrkr Voxyn Mission.
